title: filter_with_quantile
description:
Filter observations or features from an .h5mu file based on quantile thresholds of numeric values.

This component removes observations or features that fall outside specified quantile ranges in .obs or .var. columns.

For example, you can filter out observations with total UMI counts in the bottom 5% (quantile < 0.05) or 
top 5% (quantile > 0.95), or retain an intermediate range (0.1 < quantile < 0.9).

Dataset input.title: Dataset input
Dataset input.description: Dataset input using nf-tower "dataset" or "data explorer". Allows for the input of multiple         parameter sets to initialise a Nextflow channel.

param_list.description: Dataset input can either be a list of maps, a csv file, a json file, a yaml file, or simply a yaml                 blob. The names of the input fields (e.g. csv columns, json keys) need to be an exact match with the workflow input parameters.

modality.description: Which modality from the input MuData file to process.

obs_column.description:
Name of the column in .obs to use for quantile-based filtering.
If not specified, no .obs filtering will be performed.

var_column.description:
Name of the column in .var to use for quantile-based filtering.
If not specified, no .var filtering will be performed.

obs_name_filter.description: In which .obs slot to store a boolean array corresponding to which observations should be removed.

obs_log1p_column.description:
Name for the column where log1p transformed obs values will be stored.
If not provided, defaults to 'log1p_{obs_column}'.

var_log1p_column.description:
Name for the column where log1p transformed var values will be stored.
If not provided, defaults to 'log1p_{var_column}'.

var_name_filter.description: In which .var slot to store a boolean array corresponding to which variables should be removed.

output_compression.description:
Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata H5 files.
By default no compression is applied.

do_subset.description: Whether to subset before storing the output.

obs_log1p_transform.description:
Apply log1p transformation to the obs_column values before calculating quantiles.
The transformed values will be stored as 'log1p_{obs_column}' in .obs.
This is useful for highly skewed data where log transformation can improve quantile-based filtering.

var_log1p_transform.description:
Apply log1p transformation to the var_column values before calculating quantiles.
The transformed values will be stored as 'log1p_{var_column}' in .var.
This is useful for highly skewed data where log transformation can improve quantile-based filtering.

min_values_for_quantile.description: Minimum number of values required to apply quantile filtering

nextflow input-output arguments.title: Nextflow input-output arguments
nextflow input-output arguments.description: Input/output parameters for Nextflow itself. Please note that both publishDir and publish_dir are supported but at least one has to be configured.
